definition: A contract that lets software components request capabilities or data from each other.
A bundled set of libraries tools and documentation for building against a platform.
A text-based interface for operating software through commands and arguments.
A practice of automatically integrating and checking code changes with repeatable validation.
A practice of preparing or releasing software changes through automated delivery workflows.
A protocol pattern for exposing tools resources and prompts to AI applications.
A pattern that grounds generated answers with retrieved external context.
A vulnerability where untrusted input changes the meaning of a SQL query.
A vulnerability where untrusted content executes script in another user's browser context.
A Unicode character that can be visually mistaken for another character in a security-sensitive context.
A source-code attack pattern that uses directional Unicode controls to make code display misleadingly.
